title: Double Desk with Hollow-Core Door Top
summary: A two-person desk built from a hollow-core interior door laid flat on a 2x2 leg frame with a center storage cubby — no tabletop glue-up required.
description:
Using a hollow-core door as the top is what makes this fast to build: it's already flat, already finished on both faces, and already exactly as wide as most doors run (24"), so there's no panel glue-up, no edge-banding, and no sanding a tabletop smooth. The 2x2 leg frame underneath is just pocket-hole box construction, and the plywood center cubby gives both people a shared storage bay between their working areas.

The leg frame is really four separate leg assemblies joined by short 2x2 runs, which is worth understanding before you start: build the four corner units first, then the two frames that use them get connected to each other through the center storage bay. Getting the two center leg sets square to each other matters more than getting each individual leg set perfect, since that's the seam the door has to rest across evenly.

tools.note: For cutting the plywood or MDF center panel to size.

steps.title: Build the four leg sets
steps.body: Drill a 1-1/2" pocket hole on each end of every 2x2 except the legs themselves. Build four identical leg sets using glue and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add shelf trim to two leg sets
steps.body: On two of the four leg sets, add the shelf trim pieces that will support the center cubby panels.

steps.title: Join the two center leg sets
steps.body: Attach the two center leg sets together using the shelf trim pieces, forming the shared center storage bay between the two desk positions.

steps.title: Fit the center cubby panels
steps.body: Measure and cut the plywood or MDF panels to size with a circular saw. Drill 3/4" pocket holes around all sides and attach inside the 2x2 openings with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the remaining leg sets
steps.body: Attach the two outer leg sets to the center storage assembly with additional 2x2 connectors, completing the full base frame.

steps.title: Set the door on top
steps.body: Place the hollow-core door on top of the frame. Attach it from underneath with a handful of leftover 2-1/2" pocket hole screws — you only need a few, spaced around the frame's top edges.

images.alt: Double desk built from a hollow-core door on a 2x2 leg frame
